items.title: GitHub Copilot CLI adds automatic model selection for faster command-line workflows
items.summary: GitHub made Copilot auto model selection generally available in Copilot CLI so the command line can route requests to the most efficient model by default.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: model routing is becoming a built-in part of developer tooling instead of a manual setting teams have to tune on every task.

items.title: GitHub CLI adds agent skills commands for managing coding-agent setups
items.summary: GitHub launched new gh skill commands in GitHub CLI to help developers discover, install, and manage reusable skills for coding agents.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: teams are starting to treat agent skills like portable infrastructure that can be shared, updated, and audited across environments.

items.title: Anthropic opens Claude Design for polished slides, prototypes, and one-pagers
items.summary: Anthropic introduced Claude Design in Labs as a new way to collaborate with Claude on visual deliverables including prototypes, slide decks, one-pagers, and other polished assets.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: frontier assistants are moving beyond chat into structured creative output that aims to replace more of the blank-page workflow.

items.title: Anthropic ships Claude Opus 4.7 with stronger software engineering performance
items.summary: Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.7 as its latest frontier model, highlighting measurable gains on harder software engineering tasks and advanced coding workloads.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: top model vendors are still using coding benchmarks and difficult engineering work as the clearest proof point for frontier progress.

items.title: Google brings AI Mode into Chrome for side-by-side web exploration
items.summary: Google added a new AI Mode experience in Chrome so users can explore the web and AI responses together in a more integrated browsing flow.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: browser-native AI is becoming a distribution advantage as search, browsing, and assistant behavior continue to merge.

items.title: Google launches Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS for more expressive AI speech
items.summary: Google rolled out Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS across its products, positioning the model as a next-generation speech option with more expressive and controllable voice output.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: voice quality is increasingly a product differentiator as AI assistants expand into audio-native workflows.

items.title: Google packages summer travel planning and deal finding into AI-powered Search flows
items.summary: Google highlighted new AI-powered travel planning features that help users compare deals, plan trips, and explore destinations inside Search and related products.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: consumer AI wins are increasingly about getting useful workflow upgrades into familiar products people already open every day.

items.title: MIT-backed OpenProtein opens AI protein-design tools to more working biologists
items.summary: MIT News highlighted OpenProtein.AI, a no-code platform that gives biologists broader access to AI-assisted protein engineering and research workflows.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: AI biology products are moving from expert-only labs toward broader scientific teams that need simpler interfaces and faster iteration.

items.title: Salesforce turns its stack into Headless 360 APIs, MCP tools, and CLI actions for agents
items.summary: Salesforce introduced Headless 360 so more of its platform can be accessed through APIs, MCP tools, and CLI commands that agents can invoke directly.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: enterprise platforms are racing to become agent-ready backends instead of forcing every workflow through a browser UI.

items.title: Salesforce adds new Agent Fabric controls for multi-vendor AI governance
items.summary: Salesforce expanded Agent Fabric with new determinism and governance controls designed to help enterprises monitor trusted tools across multi-vendor agent deployments.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: large companies want agent interoperability, but only if they can keep execution paths observable, governed, and policy-compliant.

items.title: OpenAI expands its cyber defense ecosystem with grants, partners, and trusted access
items.summary: OpenAI outlined a broader cyber defense push spanning grants, security partners, and a trusted-access structure meant to widen defensive use while preserving safeguards.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: frontier model providers are turning security access and ecosystem design into a product surface of their own.

items.title: OpenAI previews GPT-Rosalind for life sciences research and drug discovery
items.summary: OpenAI introduced GPT-Rosalind as a purpose-built reasoning model for biology, drug discovery, and translational medicine with broader scientific tool support.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: model vendors are increasingly building domain-specific systems for regulated, tool-heavy research workflows instead of shipping one general model for everything.
